seoTitle: How to Export a Microsoft Loop Page (2026)
metaDescription: Export Microsoft Loop pages to PDF or Word so you can keep offline backups or share content outside the live workspace.
intro: Loop pages live online by default, which isn't ideal when you need an offline backup or a file to send outside your workspace. This guide covers exporting a Loop page to PDF using the browser's print function, and converting content into a Word document as an alternative.

steps.heading: Open the page you want to export
steps.body: Navigate to the specific workspace page you want to save as a file.

steps.heading: Open the more options menu
steps.body: Click the three dots icon in the top right corner of the screen to reveal the export options.

steps.heading: Export as PDF
steps.body: Select the Print and PDF export command to trigger your browser's print preview, set the destination to 'Save as PDF,' and toggle background graphics on to preserve colors.

steps.heading: Or convert to Word
steps.body: Highlight the canvas content, copy it, and paste it into Word on office.com to move the layout into a standard document format.

steps.heading: Fix missing elements
steps.body: If some elements are missing from the export, clear your browser cache and reload the page before exporting again.

steps.heading: Save and verify the file
steps.body: Download the finished document and check that all data blocks loaded correctly across every page.

faq.question: Can I export a Loop page without an internet connection?
faq.answer: No, you need to be online to load and export the page, since Loop content is stored in the cloud.

faq.question: Will tables export correctly to PDF?
faq.answer: Wide tables can clip off the page margins, so it's worth adjusting your print orientation and scaling before exporting.

faq.question: Why are some fields missing from my exported PDF?
faq.answer: This is often caused by cached data — clearing your browser cache and reloading before exporting usually fixes missing fields.

faq.question: Can I export just one section of a page instead of the whole thing?
faq.answer: You can highlight and copy a specific section into another document, though the built-in PDF export tool generally exports the whole page.
